info.name: Hyperion ERP API
info.description:
Multi-tenant commodity trading ERP - backend API (apps/api).

## Environments / variables
- `baseUrlRoot` — origin only, e.g. `http://localhost:3000` (used by /health, which is mounted outside /api/v1).
- `baseUrl` — `{{baseUrlRoot}}/api/v1`, used by everything else.
- `accessToken` / `refreshToken` — tenant user session. Auto-populated by the *Auth > Login* and *Auth > Refresh* requests' Tests scripts - just run Login once and every other tenant request in this collection picks it up automatically.
- `platformAccessToken` / `platformRefreshToken` — same auto-capture, but from *Platform Admin / Auth > Login*.
- `tenantCode` — a tenant's slug. Required on login/invite-accept endpoints when there's no subdomain to resolve it from (i.e. always, on localhost).

## Two completely separate auth worlds
Platform-admin endpoints (`/platform/**`) and tenant endpoints (everything else) are signed with different JWT secrets. A platform-admin token is rejected by tenant routes and vice versa - don't mix `accessToken` and `platformAccessToken` up.

## Conventions worth knowing before you poke at this
- Money/quantity/rate fields are always **decimal strings** on the wire (`"25.500000"`), never JSON numbers - see Purchase > Add purchase item.
- List endpoints are paginated: `{ items, total, page, pageSize }`.
- Nothing is ever hard-deleted; "delete"/"deactivate" endpoints soft-delete or flip `isActive`.
- Most POST/PATCH bodies are `.strict()` - an unrecognized field is a 422, not a silently-dropped no-op.

item.name: Health

request.description: Liveness probe. Not versioned under /api/v1 - mounted at the app root.

item.description: Unauthenticated. Note the URL has no /api/v1 prefix (see baseUrlRoot variable).

item.name: Platform Admin / Auth

request.description: Rate-limited by IP (platformLoginIpRateLimiter). On success, sets a JWT signed with PLATFORM_JWT_SECRET - a completely separate secret/signing path from tenant auth tokens, so one can never be replayed against the other's endpoints.
Rotates the refresh token. Reusing an already-rotated refresh token revokes the whole token family.
Revokes the refresh token family and denylists the current access token's jti.
Returns the calling platform admin's own profile.

item.description: Platform-admin authentication - entirely separate from tenant user auth below (different JWT secret, different login endpoint, different rate limiter).

item.name: Platform Admin / Tenants

request.description: Static module catalogue (key + name), no tenant context - used to populate the tenant-creation form's module multi-select.
Metadata + aggregate module/user counts per tenant. Never a browse of tenant business data.

Creates the tenant schema, runs tenant migrations, seeds roles/menus/field-definitions/number-series/reference-masters, creates the first company + branch, enables the requested modules, and emails the admin an invite link. Idempotent on slug: re-running with the same slug on an already-active tenant is a no-op (200, not 201). `slug` must be lowercase letters/numbers/hyphens only.
Tenant metadata + its enabled modules. 404 if unknown.
409 if the tenant is not currently active. A suspended tenant's users can no longer log in.
409 if the tenant is not currently suspended.
The full module catalogue, each entry flagged enabled/disabled for this specific tenant.
Toggles one module on/off for the tenant. 404 if moduleKey isn't in the static catalogue.

item.description: All routes require a platform-admin bearer token (never a tenant user token - enforced by a distinct JWT secret).

item.name: Auth

request.description: `identifier` accepts email OR mobile (provisioned ops users have no email). `tenantCode` is resolved from the request's subdomain in production; on localhost there is no subdomain to infer it from, so pass it explicitly here even though the field is technically optional. A must-change-password account gets back ONLY an access token scoped to "password_change" (no refreshToken) - see mustChangePassword in the response.
Rotates the refresh token. Reusing an already-rotated refresh token revokes the whole token family (all sessions for that login).
Revokes the refresh token family and denylists the current access token's jti.
The calling user's own profile (id, email, name, companyId, status).

item.description: Tenant end-user authentication. All non-login/refresh routes require the tenant bearer access token.

item.name: Users & Invitations

request.description: Requires users.user.create permission. `.strict()` body - sending `password` here is REJECTED (422), not silently dropped: admins never set a new user's password, only the invitee does via accept-invitation. Emails an invite link valid 72h, single-use.

For accounts with no email (e.g. warehouse/ops staff) - admin sets a temp password directly instead of an email invite. Forces must_change_password on first login. 403 if any of `roles` carries a financial-approval permission (self-set credentials required for approvals).
Issues a new token + expiry for a still-pending invitation and re-sends the email. 409 if the invitation isn't pending.
Invalidates a pending invitation so its token can no longer be accepted. 409 if not pending.
The one endpoint a "password_change"-scoped token (see Login) is allowed to reach - clears must_change_password and returns a fresh full-scope token pair. Works for a normal full-scoped session too.

Unauthenticated - the invitee has no session yet. `tenantCode` is required on localhost (no subdomain to resolve the tenant from); returns the invitee's email + company name so the accept-invite screen can render "You've been invited to join X".
Sets the invitee's password and activates the account. The token is single-use and expires 72h after issue.

item.description: Admin-authenticated user management, plus the two public (unauthenticated) invitation endpoints an invitee hits before ever having a session.

request.description: Renders the left-nav tree for the calling user - which modules/menu items they can see, already filtered by tenant module-enablement + RBAC. Never gated behind its own module toggle (it's the mechanism that reports what's available at all).

item.name: Get field definitions for a module/entity

request.description: Drives the schema-driven form renderer: GET /field-definitions/:module/:entity -> <SchemaForm/>. `:module`/`:entity` are e.g. "purchase"/"purchase", "suppliers"/"supplier".

Tier 2 configurable-field override only: label/visibility/mandatory/sort order. `.strict()` body - dataType/fieldKey/tier/isSystem are structurally absent, never overridable, so sending them is a 422.

item.name: Masters (reference data)

request.description: Paginated, searchable, filterable by isActive. Requires masters.country.read.
Lightweight { value, label } list of active rows only - what a form's <Select/> binds to.
Requires masters.country.create. code+name are unique per company while not soft-deleted.
Requires masters.country.update. Partial update - only sent fields change.
No hard delete (CLAUDE.md rule 8) - deactivating hides it from option/dropdown lists while preserving existing references.

item.description: One generic CRUD pattern (core/masters/factory.ts) instantiated 15 times. Every master shares the same 7-endpoint shape: list, options, get, create, update, activate, deactivate. Only Countries is annotated in full detail below; the rest follow byte-for-byte the same contract with a different URL segment.

request.description: multipart/form-data, not JSON - exactly one file part. Scanned by ClamAV before acceptance (infected -> rejected + audited). Content-type/size enforced against an allowlist. :entity/:entityId/:fieldKey identify what the file attaches to (e.g. supplier/<id>/taxCertificate).
Returns a short-lived presigned MinIO/S3 URL - never proxies the file bytes through the API itself.
FR-006. Requires suppliers.supplier.read.
FR-001..FR-005. code is auto-generated via the numbering engine - never sent. name must be unique among non-deleted suppliers (FR-005, 409 on duplicate). contacts/banks are optional nested arrays.
FR-003. Partial update. NOTE: when `contacts` or `banks` is present, the ENTIRE collection is replaced (old rows soft-deleted, new ones inserted) - not merged item-by-item.
Soft delete only (deleted_at) - never a hard delete. Frees the supplier's name for reuse (FR-005's uniqueness is WHERE deleted_at IS NULL).
FR-004. An inactive supplier is hidden from options/dropdowns but existing purchases referencing it are unaffected.
Requires purchase.po.read. status filter: draft | approved | posted.

FR-101. purchaseNumber is auto-generated (gapless, via number_series + SELECT FOR UPDATE in the same transaction) - never sent. status starts at "draft" and is exclusively system-controlled (workflow engine, not yet built). Header + Supplier Details + nested Shipment Details created together.

All fields optional - only what's sent changes. `shipment`, when present, is a PARTIAL MERGE into the existing 1:1 shipment row (unlike suppliers' contacts/banks, which replace wholesale). Blocked once the purchase is posted (posted documents are immutable - correction is reversal + re-entry).

FR-104/FR-105/FR-106. quantity/rate/exchangeRate travel as DECIMAL STRINGS, never JSON numbers (CLAUDE.md rule 1) - do not send these as bare numbers. purchaseAmountUsd/purchaseAmountAed are server-calculated and never accepted here.
All fields optional. Same decimal-string rule as Add purchase item.

variable.description: Paste the token from an invite email's /accept-invitation/:token link.
